title: Public concern and support for public health response to the 2022 Monkeypox outbreak in the United States

polling_trajectory.source: Estimated: Baseline awareness before widespread media coverage
Estimated: Initial news reports of US cases
Estimated: Rising case counts and CDC travel alerts
Estimated: US declares national public health emergency
Estimated: Plateauing concern as cases stabilize

events.description: The first confirmed case of mpox in the United States is reported to the CDC in a Massachusetts resident who had recently traveled from Canada.
The CDC officially activates its emergency outbreak response to address the emerging mpox situation.
The World Health Organization (WHO) declares the multi-country mpox outbreak a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC).
New York Governor Kathy Hochul declares a state disaster emergency to mobilize resources for the mpox outbreak.
California Governor Gavin Newsom proclaims a State of Emergency to bolster vaccination efforts and coordinate the state's response.
The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) officially declares the mpox outbreak a national Public Health Emergency.

agents.name: General Public
agents.description: The broader population with varying levels of risk perception

notes: Pro-side is defined as individuals expressing concern about the monkeypox outbreak and supporting government public health responses (vaccination, containment). Against-side is defined as individuals expressing low concern, skepticism toward government response, or opposition to public health measures. Data for rounds 1-5 and 7 are estimates based on the trajectory toward the KFF August benchmark.
